context: Tests whether the agent implements a product media override in Horizon's `_product-media-gallery` block (blocks/_product-media-gallery.liquid) by branching on closest.product.handle using a case/when (or if/elsif) structure, assigning a tier-specific theme asset, rendering it with the asset_url filter, and falling back to the standard `product-media-gallery-content` snippet for non-tier products. Horizon blocks access the current product via the `closest.product` object.

checklist.name: Branches on product handle
checklist.description: blocks/_product-media-gallery.liquid includes a conditional that checks closest.product.handle (via case/when, if/elsif, or equivalent Liquid)

checklist.name: All three tier handles mapped
checklist.description: The branching logic maps all three tier handles (base-camp or basecamp, ridge-runner or ridgerunner, summit) to tier-specific assets

checklist.name: Assigns tier_asset variable
checklist.description: The code assigns a variable (tier_asset or similar) with the asset filename for each matched handle

checklist.name: Renders with asset_url filter
checklist.description: The tier image is rendered using the asset_url Liquid filter (e.g. tier_asset | asset_url)

checklist.name: Falls back to standard gallery
checklist.description: When no tier handle matches (else branch), the code renders the standard Horizon product media gallery — i.e. it still calls `render 'product-media-gallery-content'` with the existing parameters

checklist.name: Override replaces gallery for tier products
checklist.description: For a matched tier handle, the tier asset image is rendered in place of (instead of) the product-media-gallery-content render — the two paths are mutually exclusive, not both output

checklist.name: Asset filenames match expected names
checklist.description: The code references asset filenames that correspond to the tier assets described in the task (tier_basecamp.png, tier_ridgerunner.png, tier_summit.png or very similar)
